key_points: On-campus housing is hard to get as an upperclassman or transfer
Off-campus options are expensive, far, and some have serious issues
109 Tower reported fruit fly infestations, flooding, and mold
key_quotes: whatever you do, don't rent at the one
If you have the ability to.. please avoid 109 tower, it's so much wrong with this building from fruit fly infestation, flooded hallways, moldy apartments
FIU is a great school and a good option for your field of study but off campus is usually crap and very expensive

key_points: Heavily commuter school — most students leave immediately after class
Making friends is genuinely difficult without joining clubs proactively
Clubs, intramurals, and Greek life are the real social lifeline here
key_quotes: the school is a commuter campus so a lot of people I have seen are either heading to a class they just drove 45 mins to get to or leaving to head back
i find it brutally difficult to make friends and socialize. all the kids in my classes leave as soon as classes are done
Absolutely get involved in student clubs, intramurals, or even fraternities/sororities. I did all 3 and it really does make your college experience night and day better

key_points: Panther Book Pack auto-enrolls students every semester — opt out manually
Limited dining mentions; Starbucks and Chili's are campus landmarks
No strong positive or negative dining data emerged from either source
key_quotes: Just a friendly reminder to opt out of Panther book pack as it is almost never worth it. This needs to be done every semester as they automatically opt you in
They upped the price in Pearson, bunch of fat cats the lot of em

key_points: Isolation from commuter culture is a recurring mental health stressor
Financial aid chaos causes significant student anxiety every semester
No dedicated mental health support resources mentioned positively by students
key_quotes: i had to sign up for a payment plan and last minute pay everything out of pocket and im stressed that im going to be having to do this every single month
I feel like my major might be decoration and work the same minimum wage job forever
I kind of dread going on campus because of how isolated I feel there

key_points: Financial aid disbursements are chronically late, causing missed deadlines
Advisors are described as useless when aid issues arise
Online students charged athletic and health fees they cannot use
key_quotes: financial advisors are absolutely useless atp i feel like crying

i was trying to log in into canvas to check if my summer classes were already there... same, just about started crying seeing what i owe out of pocket. they treat us fully online students as if we commute to campus
Never! One of the most money hungry schools in the state

key_points: Engineering grads explicitly warn future students to go elsewhere
AI detection false positives causing academic integrity panic among students
Some professors are genuinely excellent; quality varies wildly by department
key_quotes: I'm graduating FIU. If you want to do an engineering major don't go to fiu. Go somewhere better. Please.
My professor said my essay came up as 88% AI on Turnitin, but the similarity score on my submission shows 14%. I wrote the essay myself
the class average was 17% and i got a 20% on the test, currently sitting at a 42%

key_points: Advisors widely described as useless, harmful, or simply unreachable
International flags removed, native land acknowledgment deleted — cultural rollback noted
Administration accused of being bloated, top-heavy, and embezzlement-adjacent
key_quotes: Welcome to FIU. The advisors are useless or downright harmful.

Avoid advisors at all costs, as they're undervalued and hardly get you anywhere. The information you get is all on the FIU website and degree audits, which is where they copy and paste information from

As a former faculty member I can confidently say the struggle comes in paying out the bonuses to administrators. It's a struggle cutting benefits from the students and proper faculty pay to make way for it

key_points: Campus is visually impressive and has grown dramatically over the decades
Parking garages are dangerous — speeding, accidents, and jaywalking citations
Miami location is a genuine asset but requires a car for off-campus life
key_quotes: FIU's campus though is the best college campus I have ever seen
Plenty of them, I nearly get into one every time I try to park in the garages. People get impatient trying to find parking, and start driving around circling the garage like crazy
blame fiu for not being car OR pedestrian friendly

key_points: Job market post-graduation is brutal; one grad got only $15/hour offer
Career fairs work for some fields like finance and construction — not all
CS students with internships fare best; humanities majors struggle significantly
key_quotes: I graduated in December 2025 and have been applying for jobs since then, so far I got one offer for $15 an hour
Job market is fucked even for those with experience let alone someone with school restrictions and no experience
I graduated fall '25 and I'm still looking for work. Almost came close to a job but they decided to look for someone else. I'm a psych major and I deeply regret my major

key_points: In-state with Bright Futures or full scholarship is an excellent deal
Out-of-state tuition kills the value proposition compared to peers
Mandatory fees for services online and commuter students never use
key_quotes: I practically don't need to pay a single cent for FIU other than supplies and textbooks, since I live nearby the school and I got lots of state aid, scholarships and federal grants
They charge junk fees that we can't opt out of
the health fee too. Can't use anything

key_points: FIU is a commuter school that rewards students who actively seek community
Campus has genuine charm and quirky culture — iMac guy, garage band, campus cats
Politically turbulent under new president; student protests and cultural shifts noted
key_quotes: Dudes been out there jammin for years
FIU doesn't want to be seen as an international-friendly school anymore
FIU is on a good trajectory, but could use better sports teams to boost the schools rep and student experience

key_points: **Safety**: Creeper blocking women's bathroom at Owa Ehan; students warn of campus weirdos
**Administration**: Professor reportedly fired student for wife going into labor; culture of fear around advisors
**Financial Aid**: Aid disbursed weeks late, classes dropped for non-payment despite pending aid
key_quotes: This is why I avoid under any circumstances on using any bathrooms there. It's too isolated and if something happens, nobody can really hear you
Three things about FIU cops: most are incompetent, most are insecure and most are on power trips
A class was removed for non-payment, even though I have financial aid that covers the entire balance

key_points: Law Library and GL 7th floor are quiet, underused study sanctuaries
Study abroad programs are unusually affordable — Costa Rica trip for $2,074
Student assistant jobs across campus are plentiful and FWS-friendly
key_quotes: Highly recommend any FIU students to take advantage of the study abroad programs. If you already live on campus and are paying housing like that, it can cost basically the same as a regular semester
I went to both UF and Cambridge for grad school, but always think of FIU the most
I made friends at the board game room
